title: Buttery Fluffy Tunisian Lamb Tagine
description: A delicious and aromatic Tunisian dish featuring ground lamb, fluffy couscous, and a blend of spices, perfect for a date night. This recipe is diabetic-friendly and serves two people.
instructions: In a large Dutch oven, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat.
Add the ground lamb and cook until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the ground cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper, and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the chicken broth and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes.
While the lamb is cooking, prepare the couscous according to package instructions.
Fluff the cooked couscous with a fork and stir in the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter.
To serve, place the couscous on a plate, top with the lamb mixture, and garnish with chopped parsley and pitted dates.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
